name: USA
bio: The host nation looks to impress on home soil. With a talented young squad led by Pulisic, the USMNT aims to reach the knockout rounds in front of passionate home fans.

name: Turkey
bio: Turkey's golden generation, led by the electrifying Arda Güler, is ready to shake up the tournament. The Crescent Stars have the quality to cause major upsets.

name: South Africa
bio: Bafana Bafana represent the African continent with pride. Known for their physical style and passionate supporters, South Africa aims to recreate the magic of 2010.

name: New Zealand
bio: The All Whites make their World Cup return as OFC's representatives. Inspired by their Co-Ed team's 2023 Women's World Cup run on home soil, New Zealand are determined to compete.

name: Argentina
bio: Reigning World Champions. Argentina arrive in North America as the team to beat. With Messi potentially playing his last World Cup, La Albiceleste are driven by legacy and destiny.

name: Ecuador
bio: Ecuador punch above their weight at every tournament. With Moisés Caicedo controlling the midfield, La Tri are physical, aggressive, and dangerous on the counter.

name: Nigeria
bio: The Super Eagles bring explosive attacking talent to 2026. Led by prolific striker Victor Osimhen, Nigeria have the firepower to go deep in the tournament.

name: Indonesia
bio: Indonesia's fairytale qualification story captivated Asia. Under Korean tactician Shin Tae-yong, the Garuda are making their first World Cup appearance and ready to make history.

name: France
bio: Les Bleus possess the deepest squad in world football. Back-to-back finalists, France are hungry to reclaim the crown after their 2022 heartbreak in Qatar.

name: England
bio: The Three Lions carry the hopes of a generation. After reaching back-to-back Euro finals, England's golden generation has one final chance to end 60 years of hurt.

name: Morocco
bio: Africa's dark horses after their historic 2022 semifinal run. The Atlas Lions under Regragui are organised, fearless, and genuinely capable of going all the way in 2026.

name: Costa Rica
bio: Costa Rica are CONCACAF's ultimate overachievers. Remember their 2014 quarterfinal miracle? Los Ticos have built a reputation for stunning giants when it matters most.

name: Germany
bio: Die Mannschaft are on a mission to restore their status as world football's dominant force. With Musiala and Wirtz forming one of Europe's most exciting midfield partnerships, Germany are back.

name: Spain
bio: Euro 2024 champions playing the most attractive football in world football. Spain's tiki-taka renaissance, powered by teenage sensation Lamine Yamal, makes them genuine title contenders.

name: Japan
bio: The Blue Samurai are Asia's most consistent performers. Japan's pressing game is world-class, and after knocking out Germany and Spain in 2022, no one underestimates them anymore.

name: Panama
bio: Panama have emerged as CONCACAF's surprise package. Tactically disciplined and physically imposing, Los Canaleros will make life difficult for every opponent in Group D.

name: Brazil
bio: The most successful nation in World Cup history with 5 titles. Under Ancelotti, A Seleção combines flair with tactical discipline. Vinicius Jr. is arguably the most dangerous player alive.

name: Colombia
bio: Colombia finished Copa América 2024 runners-up with an unbeaten run. Los Cafeteros play with passion and creativity, with Liverpool's Luis Díaz leading the charge.

name: Iran
bio: Team Melli are Asia's dark horses. Mehdi Taremi's clinical finishing and a well-organised defensive structure make Iran a dangerous opponent for any team in 2026.

name: Jordan
bio: Jordan make their historic World Cup debut after a sensational AFC qualification campaign. The Nashama are riding a wave of national pride and are ready to shock the world.

name: Portugal
bio: Portugal hope Ronaldo can finally deliver the ultimate prize before retiring. With world-class talent across every position, this could be their golden generation's last dance.

name: Netherlands
bio: The Oranje have never won a World Cup despite three finals appearances. With a new generation of talent under Koeman, the Dutch believe 2026 is finally their time.

name: Senegal
bio: Africa Cup of Nations champions and 2022 World Cup quarterfinalists. The Lions of Teranga, led by the inimitable Sadio Mané, have the quality to go deep in 2026.

name: Honduras
bio: Honduras return to the World Cup after a lengthy absence. Los Catrachos are known for their tenacity and fighting spirit, and will give everything to represent CONCACAF with pride.

name: Belgium
bio: Belgium's golden generation refuses to fade. Despite multiple near-misses, the Red Devils still possess world-class talent. De Bruyne's final World Cup campaign could be truly special.

name: Italy
bio: The Azzurri are back after missing the 2018 and 2022 tournaments. With a point to prove and the hunger of the overlooked, Italy under Spalletti could be a dangerous force in 2026.

name: South Korea
bio: The Taeguk Warriors are Asia's most dynamic team. Son Heung-min's leadership and Lee Kang-in's creativity give South Korea genuine knockout stage quality in 2026.

name: Venezuela
bio: Venezuela's first ever World Cup qualification is a historic milestone for South American football. La Vinotinto play with joy and freedom, and they have nothing to lose.

name: Croatia
bio: Croatia refuse to be written off. 2018 runners-up and 2022 third-place finishers, the Vatreni under Modrić continue to punch far above their weight. Don't count them out.

name: Uruguay
bio: Uruguay are compact, aggressive, and brilliantly organised under Bielsa. With Valverde and Núñez in their prime, La Celeste are one of the most complete squads in South America.

name: Australia
bio: The Socceroos reached the 2022 quarterfinals on penalties against Argentina. Australia's high-intensity pressing style makes them one of the most difficult opponents in the tournament.

name: Algeria
bio: Algeria return to the World Cup with a squad packed with Ligue 1 talent. Led by the mercurial Riyad Mahrez, the Fennec Foxes are among Africa's most technically gifted sides.

name: Switzerland
bio: Switzerland are Europe's most consistent knockout-stage qualifiers. Under Yakin, the Swiss combine solidity with creativity, and their experienced squad knows exactly how to win big games.

name: Mexico
bio: El Tri aim to break the curse of the Round of 16. As hosts and CONCACAF powerhouses, Mexico will have extraordinary fan support. Can they finally reach the quarterfinals?

name: Egypt
bio: Egypt are finally back and they bring Mohamed Salah — arguably the best player never to grace a World Cup stage. The Pharaohs have waited for this moment for over three decades.

name: Iraq
bio: Iraq's qualification is a triumph of resilience for a nation that has overcome incredible adversity. The Lions of Mesopotamia carry the hopes of millions onto the world stage.

name: Austria
bio: Ralf Rangnick's pressing machine has transformed Austria into one of Europe's most exciting teams. Their gegenpressing style is relentless, energetic, and incredibly difficult to stop.

name: Chile
bio: La Roja's legendary 2010s generation has one last dance. Chile's famous pressing game under Gareca still poses threats to any opponent, and veteran leadership could be decisive.

name: Cameroon
bio: The Indomitable Lions have a proud World Cup history. Andre Onana's world-class goalkeeping and Vincent Aboubakar's clinical finishing give Cameroon a solid foundation for 2026.

name: Saudi Arabia
bio: Never forget Argentina 2022. Saudi Arabia shocked the world and they have the same disruptive high-line press ready to cause chaos again. Don't underestimate the Green Falcons.

name: Denmark
bio: Denmark are consistently Europe's most underrated team. Eriksen's remarkable comeback story continues, and the Danes' collective team spirit under Hjulmand is a genuine weapon.

name: Serbia
bio: Serbia possess some of the most devastating strikers in world football. Vlahović and Mitrović as a striking partnership is a nightmare for any defence at World Cup 2026.

name: Ivory Coast
bio: AFCON 2023 champions, the Elephants are riding a wave of confidence. Ivory Coast's blend of experienced European-based players and exciting young talent makes them formidable in 2026.

name: Qatar
bio: Qatar proved their 2022 home tournament was no fluke by qualifying for 2026. The Annabi have grown significantly as a football nation and will compete with renewed hunger and experience.

name: Poland
bio: Robert Lewandowski continues to defy age. Poland's talismanic striker is one of the greatest forwards of his generation, and his hunger to win a World Cup has never been greater.

name: Ukraine
bio: Ukraine's World Cup qualification carries enormous emotional weight for a nation at war. Playing for something far greater than football, the Blue and Yellow will give absolutely everything.

name: Tunisia
bio: The Eagles of Carthage are one of Africa's most technically polished teams. Tunisia consistently reach the World Cup and always give their opponents far more than they bargained for.

name: Canada
bio: Canada's football revolution is in full swing. Playing on home soil for the first time ever, the Canucks — led by Alphonso Davies' blistering pace — are ready to make their mark on history.
